description_long: This wall-mounted sconce features a horn-shaped body crafted from what appears to be dark, patinated metal, possibly bronze or brass. The surface is adorned with intricate relief patterns, including ribbed sections and stylized motifs that evoke classical or ancient decorative traditions. The sconce is supported by a sturdy, rectangular backplate and a short arm, both of which are also metallic and finished to match the main body. The open, flared top suggests it is designed to hold a candle or similar light source, with the interior likely hollow to accommodate the flame or bulb. The overall form is elongated and tapered, with a pointed end that adds a dramatic, ornamental touch. The finish is matte with subtle greenish oxidation, enhancing its antique appearance.
description: A horn-shaped, dark metal wall sconce with intricate relief patterns and a rectangular backplate, finished in a patinated, antique style.
description_view_0: The sconce is seen from a side angle, highlighting the horn shape, ribbed detailing, and the protruding rectangular backplate.
description_view_1: A direct view of the backplate and the open top of the sconce, showing the symmetrical relief pattern and the circular opening.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the elongated taper and the decorative motifs, with the mounting arm visible.
description_view_3: A frontal view emphasizes the flared opening, central relief motif, and the pointed lower end.
attribute_fixture_design: The fixture has a vintage, classical design with ornate relief patterns and a patinated finish reminiscent of ancient or antique metalwork.
